fr.recommendations: Ensuring the vehicle parking area is clearly marked and free of obstructions could facilitate emergency responder access and movement. Strategic placement and clear identification of parking spaces near entry points might enhance navigation and expedite response times by providing clearly defined pathways. Maintaining the landscaped areas by trimming any vegetation that may encroach onto access routes or obscure visibility could further assist responders in quickly identifying access routes and the layout of the property. These actions might support the overall efficiency of emergency operations and improve the effectiveness of on-site personnel during critical situations.

wildfire.recommendations: Managing the vegetation adjacent to the structure by maintaining a clear and defensible space can significantly reduce potential fire exposure to the building. This may involve trimming or removing combustible vegetation that is in direct contact with or close proximity to the structure. In areas with a vegetated perimeter, maintaining separation between groups of vegetation and ensuring adequate spacing between scattered trees can help to reduce fuel continuity and potential fire spread paths. Periodic inspections and proactive management of open areas within the property, as well as the vegetated perimeter, may support long-term wildfire resilience and readiness. By incorporating these practices, you could enhance property preparedness and create an environment better suited for supporting suppression operations if needed.
